frameworks.name: GABAergic inhibition / excitation-inhibition balance
frameworks.one_line: Local cortical GABA concentration as a regulator of network-level functional connectivity

frameworks.name: Resting-state functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: Intrinsic motor-network coupling measured at rest with fMRI

frameworks.name: Neuromodulation via tDCS
frameworks.one_line: Anodal tDCS used as a causal perturbation of M1 GABA and connectivity

core_question: Does perturbing M1 GABA with anodal tDCS causally change resting-state functional connectivity, and are the two changes linked?
key_assumptions: MRS-measured GABA reflects functionally relevant inhibition in M1
Anodal tDCS reliably reduces local GABA and alters cortical excitability
Resting motor-network connectivity is a stable, interpretable readout
manipulations: Anodal vs sham 1 mA tDCS over M1 for 20 minutes
Pre- vs post-stimulation measurement of GABA (MRS) and fMRI connectivity
key_findings: Baseline M1 GABA negatively correlated with resting motor-network connectivity
Anodal tDCS reduced M1 GABA concentration
Anodal tDCS increased functional connectivity in the stimulated motor cortex
Within-subject changes in GABA and connectivity were not correlated
interpretations: Baseline GABA-connectivity coupling reflects different mechanisms than the tDCS-induced changes
tDCS effects on GABA and on connectivity may be driven by distinct underlying processes
